title: Movie format for Rebuild Will they go beyond dvd
display_title: Movie format for Rebuild Will they go beyond dvd
wikitext:
[]: Movie format for Rebuild? Will they go beyond dvd?             Quote Post by JP » Sun Jul 15, 2007 11:18 am I was thinking. The final movie is supposed to be due a bit before 2009? Right? A lot can happen in a year+. Some anime companies like Funimation and Geneon have been talking about using the HD formats in the near future. Do you think Rebuild (maybe a 4 disk set) will be released in Blu-ray or hd-dvd? I think it would be a huge promoter in the format war. Especially since the PS3's bluray is whoopin the 360's hd-dvd in Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sun Jul 15, 2007 11:33 am When (and i know this is far off) they bring out Rebuild dvd to the west, i hope it's in HD-DVD, not Blu-ray. I am not forking out Â£500 for a PS3. :evil:

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jul 15, 2007 11:45 am You, uh... you know, you don't need a PS3 to play Blu-ray...

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sun Jul 15, 2007 11:49 am oh... :oops: sorry

[]:             Quote Post by Kaminoyouni » Sun Jul 15, 2007 11:51 am i heared blue ray players cost around 1000$ and with that a ps3 doesn't seem so bad(it's kinda like a cheaper bluray player).

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Sun Jul 15, 2007 12:17 pm NAveryW wrote: You, uh... you know, you don't need a PS3 to play Blu-ray...

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Sun Jul 15, 2007 12:19 pm I read somewhere (no idea where) that the original Rebuild was actually remastered in high definition, so I expect we'll see a Blu Ray release of that eventually. As for the movie Rebuild it all depends on whether or not anime has been successful in HD or not. I don't see why it won't be as animation looks stunning from what I've seen.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Jul 15, 2007 12:25 pm Come on people, this is Gainax. It will be released on every format possible.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sun Jul 15, 2007 12:29 pm Do I think they will release this on more futuristic formats than plain DVD? You can bet your ass a HD version of Eva '96 is in the works already.

[]:             Quote Post by AuthenticM » Sun Jul 15, 2007 1:17 pm Of course there will be an HD release of Rebuild and the original series. Some anime companies are already releasing titles on either HD-DVD or Blu-ray. As for the original series, it would be neat if they were to add more than just an upscale in resolution. They could add or modify some footage to make all episodes like the director's cut of the latters. Or, even better, they could redraw the whole thing from scratch Ã la Rebuild (but this is highly unlikely).

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Jul 15, 2007 2:06 pm AuthenticM wrote: As for the original series, it would be neat if they were to add more than just an upscale in resolution. They could add or modify some footage to make all episodes like the director's cut of the latters.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Jul 15, 2007 3:04 pm Brik-kun wrote: Come on people, this is Gainax. It will be released on every format possible.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Jul 15, 2007 3:07 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: Brik-kun wrote: Come on people, this is Gainax. It will be released on every format possible.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jul 15, 2007 7:24 pm AuthenticM wrote: They could add or modify some footage to make all episodes like the director's cut of the latters. Or, even better, they could redraw the whole thing from scratch Ã la Rebuild (but this is highly unlikely).

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sun Jul 15, 2007 7:26 pm I would buy it! but only for the interesting dialog of course....

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jul 15, 2007 8:09 pm @ EM: ...Um, as long as they release it as regular DVDs (especially in the states) for people who don't keep up with teh technology like me, I'd be happy.

[]:             Quote Post by BLACKANGEL32076 » Mon Sep 03, 2007 1:37 pm I just hope they don't pull that "only available on HD-DVD" like Michael Bay did with Transformers.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Mon Sep 03, 2007 1:56 pm BLACKANGEL32076 wrote: I just hope they don't pull that "only available on HD-DVD" like Michael Bay did with Transformers.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Sep 03, 2007 2:17 pm Szmitten wrote: Bay bashing is old and unjustified now.

[]:             Quote Post by AuthenticM » Mon Sep 03, 2007 11:01 pm I hope the DVD releases will be longer than the theatrical's. I don't think 4.5 hours is enough to tell Evangelion properly. Six hours should be the minimum.
